---
title: "Using the FGCZ Quarto report template"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using the FGCZ Quarto report template}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## What this package provides

`fgczquartotemplate` is a **Quarto format extension** that gives FGCZ analysis
packages (`ezRun`, `prolfqua`, `prolfquapp`, …) one shared look and feel: an SCSS
theme, the FGCZ header, tabset and figure-row styling, sensible cross-reference /
lightbox defaults, and an opt-in figure-finder toolbar. A theme change becomes a
single extension bump instead of an edit in every downstream report.

**See the layout live:**
<https://prolfqua.github.io/fgczquartotemplate/> — the documentation site
includes a full example report (tabsets, figure + callout rows, deep nesting,
lightbox) rendered with the extension. The site is built with
[altdoc](https://altdoc.etiennebacher.com/), which renders that report through
Quarto, so the FGCZ layout and its panel-tabsets appear exactly as they do
locally.

## Add the extension

Once per project, from the project directory:

```sh
quarto add prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate
```

This creates `_extensions/fgczquartotemplate/` in the project. Downstream R
packages that render reports at build or run time can instead **vendor** that
directory (e.g. under `vignettes/_extensions/`), so the styling travels with the
package and needs no `quarto add` step — this package's own
`vignettes/example-report.qmd` does exactly that.

## Use it in a report

Select the format in the report's YAML header:

```yaml
---
title: "My report"
format: fgczquartotemplate-html
---
```

then render as usual:

```sh
quarto render my_report.qmd
```

The theme, header, tabset and figure styling, and cross-reference / lightbox
defaults all come from the extension; the report needs no other FGCZ-specific
front matter.

## The figure-finder toolbar (opt-in)

The extension ships a right-edge toolbar — **🔍 Find** any figure or table,
browse them as a **🖼️ Figures** gallery, or **📥 Save** the plots as a ZIP. It is
off by default; switch it on per report with one line in the header:

```yaml
format:
  fgczquartotemplate-html:
    include-after-body: _extensions/fgczquartotemplate/fgcz-plot-finder.html
```

## What the extension contains

The `_extensions/fgczquartotemplate/` directory holds:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `_extension.yml` | Declares the `fgczquartotemplate-html` format and its options. |
| `fgcz.scss` | Theme overrides — tabset / card styling, figure rows. |
| `fgcz_header_quarto.html` | The FGCZ header, injected via `include-in-header`. |
| `fgcz-plot-finder.html` | The opt-in 🔍 Find / 🖼️ Figures / 📥 Save toolbar. |

For a worked report that exercises the tabset, figure-with-callout, and
deep-nesting patterns, see the packaged `vignettes/example-report.qmd`.
